subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: Given the pmf of a discrete random variable X. $\begin{array}{c|cccccc} x & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 \\ \hline p(x) & 0.35 & 0.20 & 0.15 & 0.15 & 0.10 & 0.05 \end{array}$ Find the median of X.
explanation: Since $\Pr(X\le 1) = 0.55$ and $\Pr(X\ge 1) = 0.65$, 1 is the median of X. In the case of a continuous random variable X, the median is the number M such that $\Pr(X\le M) = \Pr(X\ge M) = 0.5$. Generally, M is found by solving the equation $F(M) = 0.5$ where F is the cdf of X.
